name: code-patterns-practices
description: React Native coding patterns, best practices, and common solutions for mobile development. Use when implementing features or refactoring code.
capabilities: Provides custom hook patterns for extracting reusable logic from components
Shows compound component patterns for building flexible and declarative component APIs
Demonstrates render props technique for sharing component logic between components
Explains state management approaches using React Context and Zustand store
Covers data fetching patterns with async/await and proper cleanup handling
Details performance optimization using memoization, lazy loading, and error boundaries
author: BabakBar
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing React Native coding patterns. All static findings are false positives caused by the scanner misinterpreting code examples and documentation syntax as security issues. The skill has minimal tool permissions (Read, Write, Edit), contains no executable code, and poses no security risk.
valueStatement: This skill provides React Native coding patterns and best practices for building mobile apps. Use it to implement common patterns like custom hooks, compound components, and state management in your mobile projects.
limitations: Does not write or test actual code in your project files
Does not execute or run React Native applications
Does not provide backend API implementation guidance
Focused specifically on React Native, not general web React patterns

useCases.title: Implement new features
useCases.description: Apply established patterns when building new screens, components, or features in React Native apps.

useCases.title: Choose architecture patterns
useCases.description: Select appropriate state management and component patterns for mobile app architecture decisions.

useCases.targetUser: Frontend developers new to React Native
useCases.title: Learn React Native patterns
useCases.description: Understand React Native-specific patterns like safe area handling and keyboard avoiding view.

promptTemplates.title: Custom hook
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to create a custom hook in React Native for handling toggle state.

promptTemplates.title: Component API
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I implement compound components pattern in React Native for tabs navigation?

promptTemplates.title: State store
promptTemplates.prompt: What is the best pattern for managing global user state in a React Native app with Zustand?

promptTemplates.title: Performance
promptTemplates.prompt: How do I use useMemo and useCallback to optimize performance in a React Native list component?

bestPractices: Keep components small with single responsibility for easier testing and maintenance
Extract reusable logic into custom hooks rather than duplicating code across components
Handle loading and error states explicitly to improve user experience during async operations
antiPatterns: Avoid massive components over 300 lines that try to do too many things at once
Do not pass props through multiple levels (prop drilling); use Context or state stores instead
Never mutate state directly; always use state setter functions to trigger re-renders

faq.question: What React Native versions are supported?
faq.answer: Patterns work with React Native 0.71+ and Expo SDK 49+. Check compatibility with specific hook dependencies.

faq.question: Can I use these patterns with Expo?
faq.answer: Yes. All patterns are compatible with Expo managed workflow and bare React Native projects.

faq.question: How do I handle async operations safely?
faq.answer: Use cancellation flags in useEffect to prevent updates on unmounted components. Check cancelled state before setting state.

faq.question: Is my data safe when using this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. This is a read-only guidance skill. It only reads your code to provide context-aware suggestions.

faq.question: Why is my component re-rendering too much?
faq.answer: Check for inline function definitions in JSX. Use useCallback for event handlers and useMemo for expensive calculations.

faq.question: How is this different from React web patterns?
faq.answer: React Native patterns address mobile-specific concerns like safe area insets, keyboard avoiding, and touch interactions.
